purpose: Test the train-residual-derived hypothesis that the physical chessboard square pitch differs from the frozen 44.45 mm simulator pitch, and propagate the fitted playing-side dimension into scene geometry and command-driven physics replay.

rejected_predecessor.reason: failed the material-error and retained-lift gates

candidate.mechanism: isotropic physical chessboard playing-side dimension; square pitch is playing_side_m / 8
candidate.bounds_rationale: bounded 11.5 to 16.5 inch playing areas cover common compact physical boards without allowing an arbitrary affine warp
candidate.joint_parameterization: same board center, board yaw, four bounded joint zero offsets, and destination-reopen timing term as Stage D
candidate.exploratory_disclosure: The B-to-G train residual gradient motivated this parameter family, and an exploratory train-only kinematic fit showed that it was promising before this full replay gate was executed. It is not a pristine preregistered model-selection test.

train_acceptance.selection_rule: select Stage F only if every train gate passes; otherwise retain Stage D

confirmation.held_out_status: the two evaluator episodes were already opened by the Stage-D study
confirmation.reporting: run Stage D and the frozen Stage-F candidate side by side after train selection; confirmation cannot change selection
